zh.hero.title: 格点量子色动力学课题组
zh.hero.subtitle: 中国科学院近代物理研究所
zh.about.title: 关于我们
zh.about.intro1: 中国科学院近代物理研究所（IMP）创建于1957年，是一个依托大科学装置，开展重离子科学与技术、加速器驱动先进核能系统研究的基地型研究所。研究所围绕国家重大战略需求，开展重离子物理前沿研究，是国内重离子物理研究的中心。
zh.about.intro2: 格点量子色动力学（Lattice QCD）是从第一性原理出发研究强相互作用的重要理论工具。通过将时空离散化为四维格点，在大规模并行计算机上进行数值模拟，可以从量子色动力学（QCD）拉格朗日量直接计算强子的质量、结构和相互作用等物理量。
zh.about.lee: 李政道先生与格点量子色动力学有着深厚的渊源。他大力推动了中国格点QCD研究的发展，并提出了基于芯片的格点QCD计算方案，为在有限资源条件下进行格点模拟开辟了新思路。
zh.advisors.title: 导师简介
zh.advisors.sun.name: 孙鹏
zh.advisors.sun.bio: 孙鹏，中国科学院近代物理研究所研究员。主要从事格点量子色动力学研究，研究方向包括强子谱学、强子结构和强子-强子相互作用的第一性原理计算。在Phys. Rev. Lett.、Phys. Rev. D、Chinese Physics C等国际知名期刊发表多篇论文。
zh.advisors.sun.research: 强子谱学 | 强子结构 | 强子相互作用
zh.advisors.liu.name: 刘柳明
zh.advisors.liu.bio: 刘柳明，中国科学院近代物理研究所研究员。主要从事格点量子色动力学研究，研究方向包括核子结构、部分子分布函数和强子弱衰变等。在Phys. Rev. D、JHEP、Chinese Physics C等国际知名期刊发表多篇论文。
zh.advisors.liu.research: 核子结构 | 部分子分布函数 | 强子弱衰变
zh.research.title: 研究方向
zh.research.area1.title: 粲重子散射
zh.research.area1.desc: 利用格点QCD从第一性原理计算粲重子（如Λc）之间的低能散射参数，包括散射长度和有效力程，为理解强相互作用中的重味强子动力学提供理论输入。
zh.research.area2.title: 部分子分布函数
zh.research.area2.desc: 在大动量有效理论（LaMET）框架下，通过格点QCD计算核子和原子核的部分子分布函数（PDF），为高能物理实验中的强子结构研究提供非微扰理论预言。
zh.research.area3.title: 核子自旋结构
zh.research.area3.desc: 研究核子内部夸克和胶子对自旋的贡献，包括夸克自旋、胶子自旋和轨道角动量，解决困扰物理学界多年的“质子自旋危机”问题。
zh.research.area4.title: 格点QCD算法
zh.research.area4.desc: 发展高效的格点QCD数值算法和大规模并行计算技术，包括蒸馏技术（distillation）、域墙费米子算法等，提高计算精度和效率。
zh.research.area5.title: 核效应
zh.research.area5.desc: 研究原子核环境对核子内部结构的修正效应（EMC效应），通过格点QCD从夸克和胶子自由度理解核介质中部分子分布的变化。

en.hero.title: Lattice QCD Research Group
en.hero.subtitle: Institute of Modern Physics, Chinese Academy of Sciences
en.about.title: About Us
en.about.intro1: The Institute of Modern Physics (IMP), Chinese Academy of Sciences, was founded in 1957. It is a base-oriented institute engaged in heavy ion science and technology, and accelerator-driven advanced nuclear energy systems. Centered around major national strategic needs, IMP conducts frontier research in heavy ion physics and serves as China's national center for heavy ion physics research.
en.about.intro2: Lattice QCD is a powerful theoretical tool for studying strong interactions from first principles. By discretizing spacetime onto a four-dimensional lattice and performing numerical simulations on large-scale parallel computers, physical quantities such as hadron masses, structures, and interactions can be computed directly from the QCD Lagrangian.
en.about.lee: Professor T.D. Lee had a deep connection with lattice QCD. He strongly promoted the development of lattice QCD research in China and proposed a chip-based lattice QCD computing scheme, opening new avenues for lattice simulations under limited computational resources.
en.advisors.title: Advisors
en.advisors.sun.name: Peng Sun
en.advisors.sun.bio: Peng Sun is a professor at the Institute of Modern Physics, CAS. His research focuses on lattice QCD, including first-principles calculations of hadron spectroscopy, hadron structure, and hadron-hadron interactions. He has published numerous papers in leading international journals such as Phys. Rev. Lett., Phys. Rev. D, and Chinese Physics C.
en.advisors.sun.research: Hadron Spectroscopy | Hadron Structure | Hadron Interactions
en.advisors.liu.name: Liuming Liu
en.advisors.liu.bio: Liuming Liu is a professor at the Institute of Modern Physics, CAS. His research focuses on lattice QCD, including nucleon structure, parton distribution functions, and hadronic weak decays. He has published numerous papers in leading international journals such as Phys. Rev. D, JHEP, and Chinese Physics C.
en.advisors.liu.research: Nucleon Structure | Parton Distribution Functions | Hadronic Weak Decays
en.research.title: Research Areas
en.research.area1.title: Charmed Baryon Scattering
en.research.area1.desc: First-principles lattice QCD calculations of low-energy scattering parameters between charmed baryons (such as Λc), including scattering lengths and effective ranges, providing theoretical input for understanding heavy-flavor hadron dynamics in strong interactions.
en.research.area2.title: Parton Distribution Functions
en.research.area2.desc: Within the Large-Momentum Effective Theory (LaMET) framework, calculating nucleon and nuclear parton distribution functions (PDFs) from lattice QCD, providing non-perturbative theoretical predictions for hadron structure studies at high-energy experiments.
en.research.area3.title: Nucleon Spin Structure
en.research.area3.desc: Investigating the contributions of quarks and gluons to the nucleon spin, including quark spin, gluon spin, and orbital angular momentum, addressing the long-standing 'proton spin crisis' puzzle in physics.
en.research.area4.title: Lattice QCD Algorithms
en.research.area4.desc: Developing efficient numerical algorithms and large-scale parallel computing techniques for lattice QCD, including distillation techniques and domain-wall fermion algorithms, improving computational precision and efficiency.
en.research.area5.title: Nuclear Effects
en.research.area5.desc: Studying the modification of nucleon internal structure in the nuclear environment (EMC effect), understanding changes in parton distributions within nuclear media from quark and gluon degrees of freedom through lattice QCD.
